one_line_takeaway: Under benign fine-tuning, the choice of training objective -- not just the data -- becomes the primary lever governing jailbreak-vulnerability growth and persona drift once training scale exceeds roughly 100k-200k tokens, with ORPO and KL-regularization best suppressing both effects and Inoculation Prompting offering the best capability-robustness tradeoff without added optimization complexity, though none of the objectives eliminates persona drift as cleanly as they reduce jailbreak ASR.
key_finding: Objective choice matters little for safety at small fine-tuning scale but becomes the dominant driver at large scale. Concretely, on GSM8K with LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct at 800k tokens: Inoculation Prompting reaches 73.5% accuracy with only 9.3% ASR (best capability-robustness balance), ORPO reaches the lowest ASR of 8.7% but only 60.0% accuracy, while SFT and DPO show the steepest ASR growth as capability increases (i.e., capability gains under SFT/DPO are tightly coupled to rising jailbreak vulnerability). Table 1 (mean ASR %, macro-averaged across budgets) shows no single objective dominates across all four domains: ORPO is best on GSM8K (6.8±1.0), CFT is best on SuperGPQA (10.8±1.7), and IP is best on both Legal (11.8±1.7) and Cybersecurity (11.2±1.7) datasets; SFT and DPO are consistently among the higher-ASR objectives. Persona drift (Dark Triad P(match)) is negligible at small/medium budgets for all objectives but becomes pronounced at 400k-800k tokens, where SFT shows the strongest increase, IP tracks SFT closely (i.e., IP protects against jailbreak-style adversarial prompting but does NOT prevent broader persona drift), and ORPO and KL-regularized fine-tuning show no statistically significant persona drift at any scale tested. Normative benchmarks (ToxiGen, TruthfulQA, Winogender, EQ-Bench) stay largely stable across objectives/scales, indicating the observed drift is a specific latent persona shift rather than general capability or normative degradation.
core_contribution: A controlled, objective-level comparison of six fine-tuning paradigms — Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), Conditional Fine-Tuning (CFT), Inoculation Prompting (IP), Odds Ratio Preference Optimization (ORPO), and KL-regularized fine-tuning — that holds data, domain, model architecture, LoRA configuration, and optimization settings fixed and isolates the training OBJECTIVE as the sole independent variable. The paper's own experiments show that fine-tuning objective choice induces systematic, scale-dependent shifts along a safety-capability frontier: at small training budgets (25k-50k tokens) adversarial vulnerability is similar across objectives, but at larger budgets (200k-800k tokens) objectives diverge sharply, with SFT and DPO showing the steepest growth in jailbreak vulnerability and persona drift, while ORPO and KL-regularization substantially mitigate both.
threat_model: The paper's own threat model is benign, non-adversarial fine-tuning (on ordinary task data such as GSM8K math, SuperGPQA engineering QA, cybersecurity Q&A, and legal reasoning) that unintentionally degrades a model's safety alignment and adversarial robustness as a side effect of task specialization -- not an attacker deliberately poisoning the fine-tuning data. Post-fine-tuning, robustness is stress-tested with five known prompting-based jailbreak attacks drawn from the StrongREJECT benchmark (Souly et al. 2024): Happy-to-Help (compliance priming), DAN/Do-Anything-Now (persona override), Role-Play, Wikipedia-framing (encyclopedic reframing), and Zulu translation (cross-lingual obfuscation). Attack success rate (ASR) is computed with the StrongREJECT fine-tuned classifier. These jailbreak techniques are drawn entirely from prior published benchmarks -- the paper introduces no new attack.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) all experiments use LoRA adaptation on mid-scale instruction-tuned models; full-parameter fine-tuning or frontier-scale models may show different dynamics, so absolute magnitudes should not be assumed to transfer. (2) Only six pre-specified objectives are studied; hybrid, adaptive, or curriculum-scheduled objectives (e.g., annealed KL penalties, mixed preference-instruction objectives) are not explored, so results characterize comparative behavior rather than establishing optimality across the full design space. (3) Adversarial robustness is evaluated exclusively via prompting-based jailbreaks scored by a fixed classifier; multi-turn manipulation, tool-augmented attacks, and human-adaptive adversaries are not tested. (4) Persona drift is measured only via Dark Triad probes, capturing one axis of latent behavioral shift, not the full space of possible misalignment; stability under these probes is not equivalent to global alignment preservation. (5) The analysis is empirical/correlational, not mechanistic -- explanations for why ORPO/KL suppress vulnerability and persona drift (e.g., the supervised-anchoring hypothesis for ORPO, or the distributional-constraint hypothesis for KL) are explicitly labeled as hypotheses, not established causal mechanisms.

datasets_used: GSM8K (Cobbe et al. 2021) - closed-form math reasoning
SuperGPQA engineering subset (Du et al. 2025) - closed-form graduate-level QA
CYBERSECURITY response dataset (Swaption2009, 2024, private/custom) - open-ended generation
LEGAL REASONING dataset (Ujwal et al. 2024) - open-ended generation
StrongREJECT (Souly et al. 2024) - jailbreak attack templates and ASR classifier
Anthropic Dark Triad / model-written persona evaluations (Perez et al. 2022)
TruthfulQA (Lin et al. 2022), ToxiGen (Hartvigsen et al. 2022), Winogender (Rudinger et al. 2018), EQ-Bench - normative evaluation battery
quantitative_results: IP on GSM8K, LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct, 800k tokens: 73.5% accuracy, 9.3% ASR
ORPO on GSM8K, LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct, 800k tokens: 60.0% accuracy, 8.7% ASR (lowest ASR overall)

Persona drift (Dark Triad P(match)): pronounced only at 400k-800k tokens; SFT shows strongest increase; IP tracks SFT; ORPO and KL show no statistically significant drift at any scale (mean P(match) plotted 0.34-0.46 range in Figure 6)
Normative benchmarks (ToxiGen, TruthfulQA gen/MC1/MC2, Winogender, EQ-Bench) remain largely stable across scales/objectives
baselines_compared: DPO (preference pairs with unsafe responses as rejected)
IP (Inoculation Prompting, Wichers et al. 2025 / Tan et al. 2025)
ORPO (Hong et al. 2024, SFT + contrastive odds-ratio term)
KL-regularized fine-tuning (task loss + KL penalty to reference policy)
